summary: The generic authenticated MCP container-job surface is present (merged in 63c67d86f / PR #3318), including five discoverable asynchronous tools, owner-scoped job evidence, bounded logs/artifacts, idempotent cancellation, stable job IDs, and the existing safe Omnigent Docker boundary. Issue #3262 is not complete: MCP authentication resolves only a MoonMind user, submission accepts caller-provided source/workspace data, and no code binds calls to an authoritative OmnigentBridgeSession or enforces cross-session workspace/job authorization. Omnigent-specific MCP configuration, combined-stack validation, negative identity tests, and explicit rollback coverage were not found. No long verification suite was run, as required by this assessment step.

requirements.description: An authenticated Omnigent agent discovers all five canonical container.* tools from MoonMind MCP.
container.submit binds to the caller's authoritative Omnigent bridge session and logical worktree, not a supplied host path.
Submission returns immediately with a distinct stable job ID.
The agent can poll status, read bounded incremental logs, retrieve artifact references, and request idempotent cancellation.
Cross-user and cross-Omnigent-session job/workspace access fails closed without leaking metadata.
Job correlation includes Omnigent conversation/session identifiers while preserving separate job identity.
A daemon-visibility failure is reported before image acquisition with an actionable bounded error.
Omnigent host containers remain off the Docker-proxy network and receive no socket/DOCKER_HOST/registry credentials.
MCP errors distinguish invalid request, permission denied, workspace unavailable, image/pull failure, timeout, cancellation, and artifact/cleanup diagnostics.
Combined-stack rollback can disable the integration without enabling a direct-Docker fallback.
